name: Tiropetes & Variations
recipeInstructions:
MAIN RECIPE: Tiropetes - Cheese triangles
1. Prepare the bechamel sauce and allow it to cool.
2. Add feta cheese, egg yolks, grated cheese, parsley, and 3 tablespoons melted butter to the sauce. Mix well.
3. Cut filo sheets into long strips (3 inches wide) and brush with melted butter.
4. Place a teaspoon of the cheese mixture at the bottom of each strip. Fold the corner up to form a triangle. Continue folding in a triangular shape until the entire strip is folded.
5. Place triangles on cookie sheets, brushing each with butter. Bake at 375 degrees for 15 to 20 minutes, or until golden brown. Serve immediately. Triangles can be prepared ahead of time, frozen, and baked when ready to serve.
VARIATION: Meat - Kreatopetes
1. Use cooked leftover meats or sauté fresh ground beef, breaking it up with a fork into small pieces.
2. Combine meat with cheese and egg in a bowl.
3. Place a teaspoonful on filo strips, fold, brush with butter, and bake as directed for cheese triangles.
VARIATION: Fish - Psaropetes
1. Mix together seafood, bechamel sauce, parsley, and lemon juice.
2. Place a teaspoonful on filo strips, fold, brush with butter, and bake as directed for cheese triangles.
VARIATION: Spinach - Spanakopetas
1. Wash spinach, remove stems, dry completely with towels, and chop.
2. Brown scallions in half a cup of olive oil until tender.
3. Combine spinach, parsley, dill, beaten eggs, and cheese; add cooked scallions. Season lightly with salt and mix well.
4. Place a teaspoonful of the filling on filo leaves and proceed to fold. Brush with butter and bake as directed for cheese triangles.
Bechamel:
1. Melt butter in a pan, add flour, and stir until smooth.
2. Gradually add hot milk while stirring constantly until it thickens.
3. Season with salt and pepper.
